code-standards.control: Invoke when writing, reviewing, or auditing code for quality, style, or readability. Apply these standards before presenting any code. Check header comments, naming, error handling, and structure.
code-standards.v1: Invoke when writing, reviewing, or auditing code for quality, style, or readability. Check naming, error handling, and structure.

building-skills.control: Invoke when creating, updating, or validating skills. Apply conciseness principle to all skill content. Validate with `python skills/building-skills/scripts/validate.py` after edits. For context strategy (skill vs CLAUDE.md vs MCP), read optimizing-context skill instead.
building-skills.v1: Invoke when creating, updating, or validating skills. For context strategy (skill vs CLAUDE.md vs MCP), read optimizing-context skill instead.

optimizing-context.control: Invoke when advising on context engineering, writing CLAUDE.md files, setting up projects, designing agent architectures, or optimizing any mechanism that shapes Claude's input. Decision framework for choosing between Skills, CLAUDE.md, Prompts, Projects, MCP, Subagents, User Preferences, and Hooks. Optimize Claude's context for better outcomes.
optimizing-context.v1: Invoke when advising on context engineering, writing CLAUDE.md files, setting up projects, or designing agent architectures. Decision framework for choosing between Skills, CLAUDE.md, MCP, Subagents, Prompts, and Hooks.
optimizing-context.v2: Invoke when advising on context engineering, writing CLAUDE.md files, setting up projects, designing agent architectures, or optimizing any mechanism that shapes Claude's input. Decision framework for choosing between Skills, CLAUDE.md, MCP, Subagents, Prompts, and Hooks.

engineering-principles.control: Apply when designing architecture, performing root cause analysis, eliminating waste, auditing for duplication, reviewing designs before implementation, or when quality/process questions arise, reference for TPS/Lean foundations. Foundational engineering principles for structured work - software, documentation, processes, planning.
engineering-principles.v1: Apply when designing architecture, performing root cause analysis, eliminating waste, auditing for duplication, reviewing designs before implementation, or when quality/process questions arise. TPS/Lean foundations.
